info.title: Agent-Native Middleware API
info.description:
## Agent-Native Middleware API

Headless infrastructure for the B2A (Business-to-Agent) economy. This API provides four core services that give AI agents the 'hands' and 'eyes' to manipulate the digital and physical world.

### Service Pillars

- **IoT Protocol Bridge** (`/v1/iot`) — Secure, ACL-enforced protocol translation for IoT devices (MQTT, CoAP, Zigbee, etc.)
- **Autonomous Product Manager** (`/v1/telemetry`) — Ingest telemetry, detect anomalies, auto-generate pull requests to fix bugs
- **Programmatic Media Engine** (`/v1/media`) — Video ingestion, viral hook detection, reframing, captioning, and cross-platform distribution
- **Agent Communications** (`/v1/comms`) — Agent registration, structured messaging, capability discovery, and swarm task handoffs

### Authentication

All endpoints require an API key passed via the `X-API-Key` header.

### For Agents

This API is designed for programmatic consumption. See `/llm.txt` for LLM-optimized documentation and `/openapi.json` for the full spec.

post.description: Register a device for protocol bridging. The device will receive a unified REST endpoint that translates HTTP requests into the device's native protocol. Topic ACLs are enforced on every message. An empty ACL dict defaults to deny-all for maximum security.

403.description: Invalid API key or topic ACL violation

get.description: Paginated listing of all devices registered to your API key.

403.description: Invalid API key or topic ACL violation

get.description: Retrieve registration details and bridge endpoint for a specific device.

403.description: Invalid API key or topic ACL violation

delete.description: Remove a device from the bridge. All pending messages will be dropped.

403.description: Invalid API key or topic ACL violation

post.summary: Send a message to a device
post.description: Send a message through the protocol bridge to the device's native protocol. The topic must match an allowed ACL pattern. Messages to denied topics (e.g., camera feeds) will be rejected with a 403.

403.description: Invalid API key or topic ACL violation

post.description: Subscribe to messages from a device topic. Returns a webhook URL or WebSocket endpoint that agents can poll for incoming data. Topic must have READ permission in the device's ACL.

403.description: Invalid API key or topic ACL violation

post.description: Submit a batch of telemetry events for anomaly detection. Events are processed asynchronously. The Autonomous PM will analyze patterns across events to identify error spikes, latency regressions, and missing feature patterns.
Convenience endpoint for submitting one event at a time.

get.description: Retrieve anomalies detected by the Autonomous PM. Anomalies are classified by severity and category, with optional LLM-generated fix suggestions and auto-PR links.
Retrieve detailed information about a specific anomaly, including suggested fixes.

post.description: Instruct the Autonomous PM to generate a code fix for the given anomaly and optionally push it as a pull request. Use dry_run=true to preview the proposed diff without committing.

get.description: Aggregate statistics across all ingested telemetry events. Useful for agents monitoring system health at a glance.

post.description: Submit a video for the media engine pipeline. Provide either a source_url for the engine to fetch, or use the returned upload_url to push the file directly. Processing begins automatically after upload.

get.description: Check the current status of a video in the processing pipeline.

Retrieve the viral hooks detected in a processed video. Hooks are ranked by confidence_score. Each hook identifies a high-engagement moment based on speech patterns, emotional peaks, visual surprises, or audience retention signals.

schema.title: Response Get Viral Hooks V1 Media Videos  Video Id  Hooks Get

post.description: Generate reframed, captioned clips from detected viral hooks. Clips are automatically reframed to the target aspect ratio(s) and overlaid with animated captions in the chosen style. 80%% of social video is consumed muted — captions are not optional.

Push generated clips directly to social platforms via their APIs. No app is opened. Supports YouTube Shorts, TikTok, Instagram Reels, X Video, and LinkedIn Video. Set optimize_schedule=true to let the algorithm pick optimal posting windows based on historical engagement data.

get.description: Retrieve metadata and download URL for a generated clip.

post.description: Register a new agent in the communication network. The agent receives a unique ID and API key for messaging. Capabilities are used by other agents to discover and hand off tasks.

get.description: List all agents in the communication network, optionally filtered by capability.

post.summary: Send a message to another agent
post.description: Send a structured message to a registered agent. Messages are routed based on priority: critical messages are delivered immediately with aggressive retry, low priority messages are batched. If the recipient has a webhook, delivery is push-based; otherwise the recipient must poll.

get.description: Poll an agent's inbox for pending messages. Messages are marked as delivered upon retrieval. Use this if the agent does not have a webhook configured.

post.description: Confirm receipt and processing of a message.

Find an agent with the required capability and hand off work to it. This is the core swarm intelligence mechanism: instead of one 'God model' doing everything, tasks are routed to specialist agents. Returns the handoff message and target agent details.

Submit a source asset and target formats. The factory will generate multiple content pieces adapted to each format: short videos, static images, text posts, audiograms, carousels, blog excerpts, and email snippets. Processing is async.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

get.description: Check the status of a content generation pipeline.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

get.description: Retrieve all content pieces generated by a pipeline.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

get.description: Retrieve metadata and download URL for a specific content piece.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

post.description: Feed engagement data from platform APIs into the scheduling engine. The scheduler uses this data to learn optimal posting windows. More data = smarter scheduling = higher view velocity.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

get.description: View summary of ingested engagement data.

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

post.description: Generate an algorithmically-optimized posting schedule for content pieces. The scheduler analyzes historical engagement data per platform to pick the highest-engagement time slots. Posts are spread across days to avoid audience fatigue (configurable via max_posts_per_day).

403.description: Invalid API key or access denied

get.description: Returns all historical scan reports, newest first.

post.description: Deploy the security swarm to attack specified services with selected attack categories. Returns immediately with scan ID; the swarm runs asynchronously. Default: full scan of all services with all vectors.

get.description: Returns the full security scan report including all discovered vulnerabilities, severity breakdown, security score, and prioritized remediation recommendations.
Returns just the vulnerabilities from a scan, optionally filtered by severity. Machine-readable format for the Autonomous PM to auto-generate fix PRs.

post.description: Runs a fast, surface-level scan focused on CRITICAL and HIGH severity vectors only (ACL bypass, auth probes, privilege escalation). Returns the full report synchronously — use for CI/CD gates.

get.description: Returns the full API documentation in a flat plaintext format designed for LLM context windows. This is the primary entry point for AI agents evaluating whether to recommend this API. Think of this as SEO for the agentic web.
Returns a JSON index of all available documentation resources. Agents use this to navigate the doc tree and find relevant sections without parsing the entire llm.txt.

Standard agent discovery manifest following the emerging .well-known/agent.json convention. External agents fetch this to understand what this API does, how to authenticate, and what capabilities are available — similar to robots.txt but for AI agents.
Returns a machine-readable manifest of all available services, their base paths, and links to documentation. This is the first endpoint an agent should hit to understand what this API offers.
Returns 200 if the API is running. Agents should poll this before routing traffic.

capabilities.title: Capabilities
capabilities.description: List of capabilities this agent provides. Other agents use these to discover and hand off tasks.

webhook_url.title: Webhook Url
webhook_url.description: URL for push-based message delivery. If null, agent must poll.

AgentRegistrationRequest.title: AgentRegistrationRequest
AgentRegistrationRequest.description: Register an agent in the communication network.

api_key.title: Api Key
api_key.description: Agent-specific API key for sending/receiving messages. Store securely.

metrics.title: Metrics
metrics.description: Engagement metrics from platform APIs. The scheduler learns from this data.

AnalyticsIngestRequest.title: AnalyticsIngestRequest
AnalyticsIngestRequest.description: Submit engagement data to improve scheduling.

event_count.title: Event Count
event_count.description: Number of telemetry events contributing to this anomaly.

suggested_fix.title: Suggested Fix
suggested_fix.description: LLM-generated code fix suggestion in diff format.

auto_pr_url.title: Auto Pr Url
auto_pr_url.description: URL of the auto-generated pull request, if AUTO_PR_ENABLED.

AnomalyReport.title: AnomalyReport
AnomalyReport.description: An anomaly detected by the Autonomous PM from telemetry analysis.

anomaly_id.title: Anomaly Id
anomaly_id.description: The anomaly to generate a fix for.

target_repo.title: Target Repo
target_repo.description: Git remote URL. Uses system default if omitted.

branch_name.title: Branch Name
branch_name.description: Branch name for the PR. Auto-generated if omitted.

dry_run.title: Dry Run
dry_run.description: If true, returns the proposed diff without pushing.

AutoPRRequest.title: AutoPRRequest
AutoPRRequest.description: Request the Autonomous PM to generate and push a fix.

pr_url.title: Pr Url
pr_url.description: URL of the created PR (null if dry_run=true).

diff.title: Diff
diff.description: The proposed code changes in unified diff format.

AutoPRResponse.title: AutoPRResponse
AutoPRResponse.description: Result of an autonomous pull request generation.

topic.title: Topic
topic.description: The topic/channel to publish to. Must match an allowed ACL pattern. Path traversal sequences are rejected.

payload.title: Payload
payload.description: Message payload. Dict will be JSON-serialized; str sent as-is.

qos.title: Qos
qos.description: Quality of Service level (0=at most once, 1=at least once, 2=exactly once).

retain.title: Retain
retain.description: Whether the broker should retain this message.

BridgeMessage.title: BridgeMessage
BridgeMessage.description: Send a message to a device through the protocol bridge.

protocol_native_response.title: Protocol Native Response
protocol_native_response.description: Raw response from the underlying protocol, if available.

CaptionStyle.title: CaptionStyle
CaptionStyle.description: Animated caption styles (80% of social video is watched muted).

hooks.title: Hooks
hooks.description: Specific hook_ids to use. If null, uses top hooks by confidence.

max_clips.title: Max Clips
max_clips.description: Maximum number of clips to generate.

ClipGenerationRequest.title: ClipGenerationRequest
ClipGenerationRequest.description: Request to generate platform-ready clips from a processed video.

source_clip_id.title: Source Clip Id
source_clip_id.description: ID of an existing clip from the Media Engine. Mutually exclusive with source_url.

source_url.title: Source Url
source_url.description: URL of raw source content (video, audio, image).

title.title: Title
title.description: Working title for the content batch.

target_formats.title: Target Formats
target_formats.description: Output formats to generate. Each source becomes multiple format-adapted pieces.

brand_config.title: Brand Config
brand_config.description: Brand customization: colors, fonts, logo_url, watermark settings.

auto_schedule.title: Auto Schedule
auto_schedule.description: Automatically schedule distribution using the algorithmic scheduler.

ContentPipelineRequest.title: ContentPipelineRequest
ContentPipelineRequest.description: Submit a source asset for multi-format content generation.

estimated_pieces.title: Estimated Pieces
estimated_pieces.description: Estimated number of content pieces that will be generated.

ContentPipelineResponse.title: ContentPipelineResponse
ContentPipelineResponse.description: Response after initiating a content pipeline.

device_id.title: Device Id
device_id.description: Unique identifier for the device (e.g., MAC address or serial). Alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores, dots only.

protocol.description: The legacy protocol this device communicates over.

broker_url.title: Broker Url
broker_url.description: Override broker URL for this device. Uses system default if omitted.

topic_acl.title: Topic Acl
topic_acl.description: Topic-level access control list. Maps topic patterns to permissions. CRITICAL: Prevents unauthorized access to sensitive device feeds (cameras, microphones, floor plans). Empty dict = deny-all default.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata for agent consumption.

DeviceRegistration.title: DeviceRegistration
DeviceRegistration.description: Register a new IoT device for protocol bridging.

bridge_endpoint.title: Bridge Endpoint
bridge_endpoint.description: The unified REST endpoint to interact with this device via the bridge.

DeviceResponse.title: DeviceResponse
DeviceResponse.description: Response after device registration or lookup.

schedule_at.title: Schedule At
schedule_at.description: Schedule for optimal posting time. Null = post immediately.

optimize_schedule.title: Optimize Schedule
optimize_schedule.description: Let the algorithm pick optimal posting windows based on engagement data.

DistributionRequest.title: DistributionRequest
DistributionRequest.description: Push clips directly to social platforms via API.

DistributionResult.title: DistributionResult
DistributionResult.description: Result of a single platform distribution.

download_url.title: Download Url
download_url.description: Temporary URL to download the rendered clip.

GeneratedClip.title: GeneratedClip
GeneratedClip.description: A single generated clip ready for distribution.

capability.title: Capability
capability.description: The capability needed. System finds the best available agent.

context.title: Context
context.description: Context for the handoff — what needs to be done and why.

HandoffRequest.title: HandoffRequest
HandoffRequest.description: Request a task handoff to an agent with a specific capability.

MessageType.title: MessageType
MessageType.description: Standard message types for agent-to-agent communication.

PlatformAnalytics.title: PlatformAnalytics
PlatformAnalytics.description: Engagement analytics for a specific platform.

severity_breakdown.title: Severity Breakdown
severity_breakdown.description: Count per severity level: {critical: N, high: N, ...}

score.title: Score
score.description: Security score: 100 = fortress, 0 = DJI Romo vacuum.

target_services.title: Target Services
target_services.description: Which service pillars to attack. Default: all.

attack_categories.title: Attack Categories
attack_categories.description: Which attack vectors to deploy. Default: all.

auto_remediate.title: Auto Remediate
auto_remediate.description: If true, the swarm will attempt to auto-generate patches for discovered vulnerabilities.

confidence.title: Confidence
confidence.description: Model confidence in this time slot's engagement potential.

reasoning.title: Reasoning
reasoning.description: Explanation of why this slot was chosen.
examples: Historical peak engagement for short_video on tiktok: Tuesdays 7-9pm UTC

ScheduleRecommendation.title: ScheduleRecommendation
ScheduleRecommendation.description: Algorithmic recommendation for when/where to post.

earliest.title: Earliest
earliest.description: Earliest acceptable posting time. Defaults to now.

latest.title: Latest
latest.description: Latest acceptable posting time. Defaults to 7 days from now.

max_posts_per_day.title: Max Posts Per Day
max_posts_per_day.description: Maximum posts per platform per day to avoid audience fatigue.

ScheduleRequest.title: ScheduleRequest
ScheduleRequest.description: Request optimal scheduling for content pieces.

ScheduleResponse.title: ScheduleResponse
ScheduleResponse.description: Full schedule with per-content, per-platform recommendations.

message_type.description: Type of message: request, response, event, heartbeat, or handoff.

priority.description: Delivery priority. Critical = immediate retry, Low = batched.

subject.title: Subject
subject.description: Brief subject line for the message.

body.title: Body
body.description: Structured message payload. Must be JSON-serializable.

reply_to.title: Reply To
reply_to.description: Message ID this is replying to.

SendMessageRequest.title: SendMessageRequest
SendMessageRequest.description: Send a message to another agent.

events.title: Events
events.description: Array of telemetry events. Max 100 per batch. RED TEAM FIX: Reduced from 1000 to prevent memory exhaustion attacks. For higher throughput, send multiple batches.

TelemetryBatch.title: TelemetryBatch
TelemetryBatch.description: Batch submission of telemetry events for efficient ingestion.

errors.title: Errors
errors.description: Details of any events that failed validation.

stack_trace.title: Stack Trace
stack_trace.description: Full stack trace for error events.

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: Event timestamp. Server will assign one if omitted.

TelemetryEvent.title: TelemetryEvent
TelemetryEvent.description: A single telemetry event to ingest.

TelemetryEventType.title: TelemetryEventType
TelemetryEventType.description: Categories of telemetry events the system can ingest.

source_url.title: Source Url
source_url.description: URL of the source video to fetch. Mutually exclusive with upload.

title.title: Title
title.description: Title for internal tracking and metadata tagging.

language.title: Language
language.description: ISO 639-1 language code for transcription and captioning.

VideoUploadRequest.title: VideoUploadRequest
VideoUploadRequest.description: Initiate a video upload for processing.

upload_url.title: Upload Url
upload_url.description: Pre-signed URL for direct upload (if source_url was not provided).

confidence_score.title: Confidence Score
confidence_score.description: Model confidence that this segment will drive engagement.

transcript_snippet.title: Transcript Snippet
transcript_snippet.description: Text of what is being said during this hook.

ViralHook.title: ViralHook
ViralHook.description: A detected high-engagement moment in the source video.

description.title: Description
description.description: Detailed technical explanation including attack vector and impact.

evidence.title: Evidence
evidence.description: Proof-of-concept: request payload, response status, headers, etc.

remediation.title: Remediation
remediation.description: Recommended fix. Agents read this to auto-generate patches.

cwe_id.title: Cwe Id
cwe_id.description: Common Weakness Enumeration ID (e.g. CWE-285 for improper authorization).

app__schemas__telemetry__Severity.title: Severity
app__schemas__telemetry__Severity.description: Severity levels for anomalies and issues.

APIKeyHeader.description: API key for agent authentication. Pass in the X-API-Key header.
APIKeyHeader.name: X-API-Key
